mnemonics: COMPONENTS: COnnected Modules Providing Operational Needs, Easily Nestable To Streamline
keyPoints: A component is a modular, replaceable part of a system that encapsulates its contents and provides a well-defined interface.
Components enable reuse, reduce complexity, and support independent development and deployment.
They are often visualized in UML component diagrams which show the organization and dependencies between components.
commonMistakes: Confusing a component (a runtime, physical module) with a class (a design-time, logical construct).
Designing components that are too tightly coupled, making the system rigid and difficult to change or maintain.
